key_findings: Phase 1 passive enrichment (CNAME attribution, TLS SAN correlation, response header fingerprinting, error page profiling) achieves +15-25% signal gain at ~0-1% additional cost — highest ROI tier because all techniques derive value from already-collected DNS/HTTP data

CNAME chain attribution enables subdomain takeover detection (14 known-vulnerable platforms: Heroku, S3, GitHub Pages, Azure, etc.) at zero additional tool cost — O(1) string match per DNS record against fingerprint database

TLS SAN correlation extracts novel sibling subdomains from already-collected httpx TLS certificate data — average enterprise cert has 5-15 SANs; 10% novel discovery rate = significant corpus expansion without additional DNS scan budget

JS endpoint extraction (Phase 2, gated at KRIL>=60 AND ACS>=60) provides +10-15% finding density on high-value gated assets; katana already fetches JS files — structured analysis is an incremental cost on top of existing budget
Reverse DNS PTR expansion (Phase 2, gated) reveals corporate infrastructure siblings at ~1% cost increase — 1 DNS query per unique IP in high-KRIL space
ASN/CIDR scanning and HTTP smuggling probes DEFERRED — cost +20-30%, infra mutation risk, scope violation potential; nuclei templates already handle targeted versions
Combined Phase 1-2: +28-40% total signal gain at +4-7% total cost — exceeds >=25% signal target and stays within <=10% cost constraint
Enrichment findings with confidence <0.7 queued for nuclei validation, not auto-reported — prevents false positive inflation in analyst-facing views

decisions.title: Passive post-processor architecture over active tool addition
decisions.summary: All Phase 1 techniques run as stateless post-processors on already-collected data; zero new tool invocations required
decisions.rationale: Adding new tool calls scales linearly with corpus — violates <=10% cost constraint at 10M+ subdomain scale. Passive post-processors derive value from data already paid for by existing DNS/HTTP scans. The marginal cost is CPU cycles for string matching, not network I/O.

decisions.title: KRIL+ACS dual gate for active Phase 2 enrichment
decisions.summary: JS extraction and PTR expansion only run on assets with KRIL>=60 AND ACS>=60
decisions.rationale: Without gating, JS extraction on all 10M subdomains would cost orders of magnitude more than the <=10% budget allows. The dual gate ensures enrichment runs only on high-value, confirmed-live assets — the intersection where enrichment is most productive.

decisions.title: CNAME takeover fingerprint database as extensible configuration (not hardcoded)
decisions.summary: TAKEOVER_FINGERPRINTS dict versioned and updatable without code change
decisions.rationale: New platforms emerge regularly. Hardcoding fingerprints requires code deploys for updates. Externalizing the fingerprint database enables monthly updates via config file, consistent with the hot-reload philosophy of score_config.json.

decisions.title: Confidence-gated finding emission for enrichment candidates
decisions.summary: Enrichment techniques emit finding_candidates with confidence 0.0-1.0; only confidence>=0.7 auto-reported; lower confidence queued for nuclei validation
decisions.rationale: Passive techniques (header fingerprinting, error page profiling) produce indicators, not confirmed findings. Auto-reporting all indicators would inflate false positive rate. Confidence gate ensures analysts see confirmed findings while low-confidence candidates are queued for nuclei template validation.

decisions.title: ASN/CIDR scanning and HTTP smuggling deferred
decisions.summary: Both techniques violate cost constraint (>10%) or infra-mutation risk; nuclei templates cover targeted versions
decisions.rationale: ASN scanning requires O(256 × ports) probes per discovered IP — at scale this exceeds cost budget and triggers rate limits. HTTP smuggling probes at mass scale risk unintended mutation of target state. Both are better implemented as nuclei templates on explicitly targeted assets.

techniques.name: CNAME Chain Attribution
techniques.mechanism: Post-DNS-parse: match terminal CNAME against TAKEOVER_FINGERPRINTS dict; flag cname_takeover_candidate=true + cname_provider if match; queue for HTTP verification

techniques.name: TLS Certificate SAN Correlation
techniques.mechanism: Post-HTTP-parse: extract SANs from TLS cert data in httpx output; cross-reference against corpus; novel SANs added to DNS queue with source=tls_san; SAN_PEER relationship emitted in Neo4j

techniques.name: Response Header Fingerprinting
techniques.mechanism: Post-HTTP-parse: analyze stored response headers against HEADER_SIGNATURES ruleset; emit exposure/misconfiguration candidates for missing security headers, outdated server versions, CORS wildcards

techniques.name: Error Page Profiling
techniques.mechanism: Post-HTTP-parse: pattern-match 4xx/5xx response bodies against error_page_signatures; detect framework versions in error messages, stack traces, debug mode indicators, database error strings

techniques.name: JavaScript Endpoint Extraction
techniques.mechanism: Post-katana: analyze fetched .js files for API endpoint patterns (/api/, /v1/, /admin/), credential patterns (AWS keys, JWT, OAuth secrets), internal domain references
techniques.confidence_output: 0.6 for credentials (requires analyst review); 0.75 for API endpoints

techniques.name: Reverse DNS PTR Expansion
techniques.gate: KRIL >= 50 (IP must be associated with high-KRIL subdomain)
techniques.mechanism: Post-DNS: 1 PTR lookup per unique IP in gated set; PTR result cross-referenced against root domain scope; novel hostnames added to DNS queue

techniques.name: ASN/CIDR Neighbor Scanning
techniques.rejection_reason: Exceeds cost constraint; scope violation risk; infra mutation risk; nuclei templates cover targeted version

techniques.name: HTTP Smuggling Probe
techniques.rejection_reason: High false positive rate without careful validation; risk of state mutation on target; nuclei templates already exist for targeted use

implementation_architecture.execution_model: async batch, runs after each DNS/HTTP parse batch completes
implementation_architecture.finding_emission: EnrichmentResult with confidence float; auto-report if confidence >= 0.70; queue for nuclei validation if 0.40 <= confidence < 0.70

cname_fingerprint_database.verification_method: HTTP response body match against platform error string

risk_model.title: CNAME fingerprint database stale; misses new platforms
risk_model.mitigation: Version CNAME_FINGERPRINTS; monthly update; false negatives acceptable (no false positives from stale data)

risk_model.title: TLS SAN expansion creates corpus explosion
risk_model.mitigation: SAN dedup against corpus before adding; wildcard SANs stripped; rate-limit SAN additions per cycle

risk_model.title: JS extraction produces false credential positives
risk_model.mitigation: Credential candidates capped at confidence=0.6; require analyst review before reporting

risk_model.title: Error page profiling high false positive rate
risk_model.mitigation: Error page findings at confidence<=0.5; not auto-reported; queued for nuclei validation

risk_model.title: PTR results point to out-of-scope infrastructure
risk_model.mitigation: PTR results cross-referenced against root domain scope before adding to queue

risk_model.title: Phase 2 cost exceeds estimate for large gated asset set
risk_model.mitigation: Monitor cost delta as ORS signal; alert if >8%; auto-raise KRIL/ACS gate threshold

recommended_actions.action: Implement CNAME attribution post-processor with 14-platform fingerprint database — zero cost, immediate takeover candidate detection
Implement TLS SAN correlation post-processor extracting novel SANs from httpx output — zero cost, corpus expansion
Implement response header fingerprinting with HEADER_SIGNATURES ruleset — low CPU cost, pre-labels nuclei targets
Implement error page profiler for 4xx/5xx body pattern matching — catches framework version disclosure and debug mode
Externalize TAKEOVER_FINGERPRINTS and HEADER_SIGNATURES as versioned config files — enables monthly updates without code deploy
Add Phase 2: JS extraction post-katana (KRIL>=60, ACS>=60 gate) — highest absolute finding density gain on high-value gated assets
Add Phase 2: PTR expansion for IPs associated with KRIL>=50 subdomains — low cost, reveals corporate infrastructure siblings
Monitor enrichment cost delta as ORS signal after each Phase 2 cycle — alert if total enrichment overhead >8% of baseline scan budget

assumptions: A1: dnsx output includes full CNAME chain data; terminal CNAME accessible in parsed record
A2: httpx output includes TLS certificate SAN list already parsed by httpx_parser_v1
A3: HTTP response headers and body stored and accessible for post-parse analysis
A4: katana crawl output includes fetched JS file content or URL list
A5: Reverse DNS PTR lookup permissible within current network access profile
A6: CNAME fingerprint database starts at 14 platforms; extensible by config update
A7: Enrichment findings with confidence <0.7 queued for nuclei validation, not auto-reported
